title: Dentaria × degeniana
taxonomy: Dentaria × degeniana is a hybrid species. It is a cross between Dentaria bulbifera and Dentaria glandulosa. As a hybrid, Dentaria × degeniana's classification can be somewhat complex. The species is often classified as a hybrid of the genus Dentaria. This classification is due to its unique genetic makeup resulting from the cross between two different species.

intro: Dentaria × degeniana, also known as Degen's toothwort, is a rare, hybrid plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and resulting from the cross between Dentaria bulbifera and Dentaria glandulosa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Dentaria × degeniana include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into ointments and salves to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
